IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: TO ADVANCE HEALTH EQUITY IN ALAMEDA CO. BY RAISING FUNDS & SUPPORT FOR ALAMEDA HEALTH SYSTEM.
IRS990.MissionDesc: ALAMEDA HEALTH SYSTEM FOUNDATION'S MISSION IS TO ADVANCE HEALTH EQUITY IN THE COMMUNITIES SERVED BY ALAMEDA HEALTH SYSTEM (AHS).
IRS990.Desc: ENSURING HEALTHIER OUTCOMES:IN FY24, THE ALAMEDA HEALTH SYSTEM FOUNDATION (AHSF) DISBURSED FUNDS TO ALAMEDA HEALTH SYSTEM PROGRAMS AND SERVICES TO ADVANCE HEALTHIER OUTCOMES FOR AHS PATIENTS. FUNDS SUPPORTED SALARIES, EQUIPMENT, AND DIRECT PROGRAM SUPPORT. INVESTMENTS INCLUDED PATIENT SUPPORTS SUCH AS CAR SEATS AND TRANSLATION SERVICES, HELPING OVER 2,000 INDIVIDUALS ACCESS CARE WITH DIGNITY.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: ACCESS TO QUALITY CARE:AHSF PROVIDED FUNDS TO ALAMEDA HEALTH SYSTEM PROGRAMS AND SERVICES TO EXPAND ACCESS TO ESSENTIAL SERVICES, INCLUDING DIRECT SUPPORT FOR IMAGING, EMERGENCY RESPONSE, AND TRAUMA-INFORMED CARE. FUNDS ALSO COVERED EQUIPMENT PURCHASES AND DIRECT PATIENT ASSISTANCE--REMOVING BARRIERS FOR THOUSANDS NEEDING MAMMOGRAMS, PRESCRIPTION COVERAGE, AND TRANSPORTATION TO CRITICAL APPOINTMENTS.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT:AHSF INVESTED FUNDS IN BUILDING THE NEXT GENERATION OF HEALTH WORKERS AT ALAMEDA HEALTH SYSTEM. THIS INCLUDED DIRECT SUPPORT FOR SCHOLARSHIPS, STIPENDS, AND TRAINING EQUIPMENT. FUNDS SUPPORTED HEALTHPATH INTERNSHIPS, NURSING SCHOLARSHIPS, AND PROGRAMS PROVIDING YOUNG PEOPLE, FOSTER ALUMNI, AND REFUGEES WITH PATHWAYS INTO HEALTHCARE CAREERS.

Form990PartVIISectionAGrp.TitleTxt: SR DIR, DEV OPS, ANNUAL GIVING, & STEWARDSHIP

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION'S ARTWORK INCLUDES 8 ORIGINALLY FRAMED PAINTINGS WHICH WILL BE DISPLAYED IN PUBLIC AREAS AND PRESERVED FOR FUTURE GENERATIONS. THE COLLECTION ENRICHES THE ENVIRONMENT WITH CULTURAL ART IN SUPPORT OF A HEALING ENVIRONMENT, WHICH SERVES ALAMEDA COUNTY'S DIVERSE COMMUNITY.
THE ENDOWMENT FUNDS ARE UTILIZED FOR THE CARE OF POOR AND INDIGENT PATIENTS.

THE FOUNDATION IS A PUBLIC CHARITY UNDER INTERNAL REVENUE CODE (IRC) SECTION 509(A)(1) AND IS EXEMPT FROM TAXATION UNDER SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE IRC AND SECTION 23701D OF THE CALIFORNIA REVENUE AND TAXATION CODE AND IS GENERALLY NOT SUBJECT TO FEDERAL OR STATE INCOME TAXES. HOWEVER, THE FOUNDATION IS SUBJECT TO INCOME TAXES ON ANY NET INCOME THAT IS DERIVED FROM A TRADE OR BUSINESS CARRIED ON, AND NOT IN FURTHERANCE OF THE PURPOSES FOR WHICH IT WAS GRANTED EXEMPTION. NO INCOME TAX PROVISION HAS BEEN RECORDED, AS THE NET INCOME, IF ANY, FROM ANY UNRELATED TRADE OR BUSINESS, IN THE OPINION OF MANAGEMENT, IS NOT MATERIAL TO THE ACCOMPANYING FINANCIAL STATEMENTS TAKEN AS A WHOLE. THE FOUNDATION FOLLOWS THE ACCOUNTING FOR UNCERTAINTY IN INCOME TAXES RECOGNIZED IN A NONPUBLIC ENTITY'S FINANCIAL STATEMENTS. THE GUIDANCE DETAILS HOW ENTITIES SHOULD RECOGNIZE, MEASURE, PRESENT, AND DISCLOSE UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS THAT HAVE BEEN OR ARE EXPECTED TO BE TAKEN. AS SUCH, FINANCIAL STATEMENTS WILL REFLECT EXPECTED FUTURE TAX CONSEQUENCES OF UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS PRESUMING THE TAXING AUTHORITIES' FULL KNOWLEDGE OF THE POSITION AND ALL RELEVANT FACTS. THERE WAS NO IMPACT TO THE FOUNDATION'S FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AS A RESULT OF THESE PROVISIONS.
FUNDRAISING EVENT EXPENSES -396,307. LOSS ON SALE OF FIXED ASSETS -2,328.
FUNDRAISING EVENT EXPENSES 396,307. LOSS ON SALE OF FIXED ASSETS 2,328.

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: REIMBURSEMENT FOR CHARITABLE PROGRAMS OPERATED BY AHS

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: CERTAIN CHARITABLE PROGRAMS OPERATED BY AHS ARE FUNDED BY THE FOUNDATION. THE FOUNDATION REQUIRES RECORDS REGARDING THE COST AND NECESSARY MONETARY REQUIREMENTS OF THESE PROGRAMS PRIOR TO THE REIMBURSEMENT OF MONETARY AMOUNTS.

RltdOrgOfficerTrstKeyEmplGrp.TitleTxt: SR DIR, DEV OPS, ANNUAL GIVING, & ST

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE FOUNDATION IS REPORTING THE NUMBER OF ITEMS CONTRIBUTED IN SCHEDULE M, PART I, COLUMN (B).

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE FORM 990 IS PREPARED BY AN INDEPENDENT TAX PREPARER IN CONJUNCTION WITH THE ORGANIZATION'S FINANCE AND ACCOUNTING STAFF. THE FORM 990 IS THEN REVIEWED BY THE DIRECTOR OF FINANCE & OPERATIONS AND PRESIDENT PRIOR TO FILING WITH THE INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: EACH BOARD MEMBER, OFFICER, AND KEY EMPLOYEE OF THE FOUNDATION SIGNS AN ANNUAL DISCLOSURE AND COMPLIANCE STATEMENT WHICH: (I) AFFIRMS THAT THE PERSON HAS RECEIVED A COPY OF THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, HAS READ AND UNDERSTOOD THE POLICY, AND HAS AGREED TO COMPLY WITH THE POLICY, AND (II) DISCLOSES THE PERSON'S FINANCIAL INTERESTS AND FAMILY RELATIONSHIPS THAT COULD GIVE RISE TO CONFLICTS OF INTEREST. WHEN A POTENTIAL CONFLICT IS IDENTIFIED, THE BOARD SHALL DETERMINE IF A CONFLICT OF INTEREST EXISTS AND MAY DELEGATE INITIAL REVIEW AND RECOMMENDATION TO ITS BOARD GOVERNANCE/NOMINATING COMMITTEE AND/OR THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE. IF A DIRECTOR IS THE INDIVIDUAL WITH WHICH A POTENTIAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST IS IDENTIFIED, THE DIRECTOR SHALL NOT PARTICIPATE IN DISCUSSION OR VOTE IN ANY MATTER RELATED TO THE TRANSACTION. THE BOARD OR DESIGNEE COMMITTEE MEMBERS MAY ASK QUESTIONS OF AND RECEIVE PRESENTATIONS FROM THE INDIVIDUALS WITH THE POTENTIAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST AND OTHER INTERESTED PERSONS. AFTER EXERCISING DUE DILIGENCE, WHICH MAY INCLUDE INVESTIGATING ALTERNATIVES THAT PRESENT NO CONFLICT, THE BOARD OR DESIGNEE COMMITTEE MEMBERS VOTE UPON THE MATTER, THE PROCEEDINGS OF WHICH ARE RECORDED IN THE MINUTES OF EACH BOARD MEETING.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE SALARY FOR THE PRESIDENT AS WELL AS OTHER OFFICERS AND KEY EMPLOYEES IS ESTABLISHED BY THE COMPENSATION COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD, WHO UTILIZES AN OUTSIDE THIRD-PARTY SALARY STUDY TO ASSURE THAT CURRENT SALARY MEETING CURRENT MARKET GUIDELINES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS ARE MADE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.

PROFESSIONAL FEES: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 1,162,029. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 569,708. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 1,731,737. PAYROLL SERVICE FEES: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 0. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 13,088. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 13,088.
